  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
  "opinion": "As someone who's been using social media for a while, I think we need to be more careful about what we see online. You know, when I'm scrolling through Facebook or Twitter, sometimes it feels like half the stuff is made up nonsense. With my high neuroticism score, I tend to worry easily, and that fake news can really mess with your head because you're not sure if it's true or not. But Rome was not built in a day, so maybe we can build better habits bit by bit.",
  "reasoning_summary": "I started as an enthusiast but now realize social media has downsides due to my depression and high neuroticism.\n\nMy moderate agreeableness makes me want to believe people are good, which is why fake positivity bothers me less while misinformation causes more anxiety. \n\nHigh openness means I'm curious, so even though I know it's fake sometimes I can't help but engage with viral stuff that sounds exciting or profound.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As someone who's been using social media for a while, I think we need to be more careful about what we see online. You know, when I'm scrolling through Facebook or Twitter, sometimes it feels like half the stuff is made up nonsense. With my high neuroticism score, I tend to worry easily, and that fake news can really mess with your head because you're not sure if it's true or not. But Rome was not built in a day, so maybe we can build better habits bit by bit.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary:
I started as an enthusiast but now realize social media has downsides due to my depression and high neuroticism.

My moderate agreeableness makes me want to believe people are good, which is why fake positivity bothers me less while misinformation causes more anxiety. 

High openness means I'm curious, so even though I know it's fake sometimes I can't help but engage with viral stuff that sounds exciting or profound.
